name: Sourdough Starter (2 of 2)
recipeInstructions:
1. To prevent the starter from becoming rancid, use a portion of it at least once a week.
- If baking sourdough breads infrequently, remove and feed a cup of starter as needed during the week.
2. If away on vacation or unable to maintain the starter, freeze it in portions for later thawing.
3. Thaw frozen starter in the refrigerator before use.
4. Use only once a week; allow time for fermentation before baking sourdough breads.
Note: A well-maintained starter can last for years, with some lasting up to 14 years. Experiment with early morning baking and milk additions to enhance sourness.
